_note: Catalog of vendored axiom.ai user-facing docs. To pick relevant reading for a task: 1. Skim the `categories` array — each entry's `key` is the directory path. 2. Within a category, scan the `entries` array — each has a `title` and `description`. 3. Read 3-6 files whose descriptions match the user's prompt before composing an artifact. The .md files preserve the source's Nuxt Content frontmatter and `::ComponentName{}` shortcodes — read them as prose.

entries.title: Get support
entries.description: Reach out for help with an axiom.ai automation through the customer support form, the Reddit community, or a dedicated Slack channel.

entries.title: Documentation
entries.description: Transform yourself into a browser automation expert with axiom.ai's offical documentaion.

categories.description: Create an account to get 120 minutes of free runtime, manage account settings from the dashboard, and manage your subscription, billing, and invoices.

entries.title: Manage your account
entries.description: Update your axiom.ai email, password, company information, country, and team membership, or delete your account permanently.

entries.title: Manage your subscription
entries.description: Change plan, update payment method and billing details, and download invoices from the Stripe payment portal connected to your axiom.ai account.

entries.title: Create an account
entries.description: Create an axiom.ai account with Google or an email address to get 120 minutes of free runtime to start building automations.

entries.title: Account
entries.description: Create an account to get 120 minutes of free runtime, manage account settings from the dashboard, and manage your subscription, billing, and invoices.

entries.title: Dashboard
entries.description: Navigate the Code Tool dashboard, including Home, run reports, the Playground, settings, theme, and remaining runtime.

entries.title: Playground
entries.description: The Playground lets you test scripts and watch them run in a hosted browser, without setting up a local Node.js environment.

entries.description: Write Puppeteer scripts in your local environment and connect them to axiom.ai's hosted browser using your API key.

entries.title: Run scripts
entries.description: Run Code Tool scripts from your own development environment using Node.js, or test them in the axiom.ai Playground without any local setup.

entries.title: Run reports
entries.description: View in-progress and completed Code Tool runs, watch live runs, check durations, and inspect errors and run details from the dashboard.

entries.title: Generate an API key
entries.description: Generate the API key used to authenticate Code Tool scripts to axiom.ai's hosted browser, with guidance on storing it securely.

categories.description: Sample Puppeteer scripts you can run in the axiom.ai Playground or adapt for your own automations, covering navigation and data entry.

entries.title: Navigate to a page
entries.description: Sample Puppeteer script for the axiom.ai Code Tool that connects to the hosted browser, opens a page, waits, and closes the browser cleanly.

entries.title: Enter data into a page
entries.description: Sample Puppeteer script for the axiom.ai Code Tool that connects to the hosted browser, fills a search field on Google, waits, and closes cleanly.

entries.title: Sample scripts
entries.description: Sample Puppeteer scripts you can run in the axiom.ai Playground or adapt for your own automations, covering navigation and data entry.

categories.description: Three API surfaces with one key. Trigger pre-built automations over REST, connect Puppeteer or Playwright over CDP, or call high-level browser steps from Node.

entries.description: Trigger a pre-built No-Code Tool automation with cURL, or drive a cloud browser from your own code with Puppeteer.

entries.title: Authentication
entries.description: Generate an axiom.ai API key from the Chrome extension or Code Tool and use it to authenticate every API request.

entries.title: Programmatic signup
entries.description: Three-call REST flow for code that needs to sign up its own user, log in, and mint an API key — useful for agents, Claude skills, and headless onboarding.

categories.description: Drive a cloud browser from your own code over a Chrome DevTools Protocol WebSocket. Works with Puppeteer, Playwright, raw CDP libraries, or anything else that speaks the protocol.

entries.description: Drive a cloud browser from your own code over a Chrome DevTools Protocol WebSocket. Works with Puppeteer, Playwright, raw CDP libraries, or anything else that speaks the protocol.

categories.description: Drive axiom.ai from Claude, Cursor, or any MCP client. The axiom.ai desktop app ships with a built-in MCP server; alternatively, build your own in Python or TypeScript.

entries.title: Desktop app MCP server
entries.description: The axiom.ai desktop app ships with a built-in MCP server. Toggle it on, paste your API key, and Claude Desktop, Claude Code, Cursor, or any MCP client can trigger axiom.ai automations and step functions in conversation.

entries.title: Build your own server (Python)
entries.description: Build a minimal Model Context Protocol server in Python that exposes axiom.ai automation triggers as tools for any MCP client.

entries.description: Build a minimal Model Context Protocol server in Node and TypeScript that exposes axiom.ai automation triggers as tools for any MCP client.

entries.title: MCP
entries.description: Drive axiom.ai from Claude, Cursor, or any MCP client. The axiom.ai desktop app ships with a built-in MCP server; alternatively, build your own in Python or TypeScript.

entries.description: Every axiom.ai API endpoint at a glance, with method, path, required parameters, and links to full reference pages.

entries.description: Every axiom.ai API error response, what it means, and how to resolve it.

entries.title: Run automations
entries.description: Trigger pre-built No-Code Tool automations, pass input data, poll for results, and stop runs from outside the Chrome extension.

entries.title: Trigger an automation
entries.description: Start a saved No-Code Tool automation in the cloud by name, with optional input data passed in the request body.

entries.title: Pass input data
entries.description: Send dynamic input into a No-Code Tool automation when triggering it via the API, using the table-shaped data payload.

entries.title: Check run status
entries.description: Poll the axiom.ai API for the current status of an automation by name and retrieve any data it wrote out.

entries.title: List run reports
entries.description: Fetch a list of recent automation runs on your account, each with its status, description, and timestamps.

entries.title: List automations
entries.description: Fetch the names of every saved No-Code Tool automation on your account, useful for building UIs or validating an automation name before triggering it.

entries.title: Stop a run
entries.description: Cancel a triggered automation that's still running in the cloud, freeing the concurrency slot for other runs.

entries.title: Webhooks from a step
entries.description: Use webhook steps inside an automation to push data to external services or trigger an automation from inbound webhooks.

categories.description: Drive a cloud browser session step by step (goto, click, enter text, wait, scrape) choosing each action based on what the previous one returned.

entries.title: Start a session
entries.description: Install axiom-api, instantiate the AxiomApi class with your API key, then open a cloud browser session.

entries.title: Go to URL
entries.description: Send the cloud browser session to a URL using axiom.goto() and wait for the page to load.

entries.title: Click
entries.description: Click a button, link, or any other element in a cloud browser session by passing a CSS selector to axiom.click().

entries.title: Enter text
entries.description: Enter text into an input, textarea, or other focusable element in a cloud browser session using axiom.enterText().

entries.title: Click multiple
entries.description: Click every element matching a CSS selector, up to a maximum, using axiom.clickMultiple().

entries.title: Step functions
entries.description: Drive a cloud browser session step by step (goto, click, enter text, wait, scrape) choosing each action based on what the previous one returned.

entries.title: Click engagement button
entries.description: Click a like, follow, subscribe, or similar toggle only when it isn't already in the target state, using axiom.clickEngagementButton().

entries.title: Hover
entries.description: Move the mouse over an element to trigger hover-only UI (dropdown menus, tooltips, lazy-loaded content) using axiom.hover().

entries.title: Click and drag
entries.description: Press the mouse at one coordinate, drag to another, and release using axiom.clickAndDrag(). Useful for sliders, range pickers, drag-and-drop UIs, and slider captchas.

entries.title: Press keys
entries.description: Send keyboard key presses (Enter, Tab, arrow keys, modifier combinations) to the currently focused element using axiom.pressKeys().

entries.title: Select list
entries.description: Pick an option in a native HTML <select> element by visible text using axiom.selectList().

entries.title: Date picker
entries.description: Navigate a calendar widget month by month and pick a target day using axiom.datePicker().

entries.title: Switch browser tab
entries.description: Switch the cloud browser session's focus to another tab using axiom.switchBrowserTab().

entries.title: Get clipboard contents
entries.description: Read the contents of the cloud browser's clipboard, useful for pages that put their copy output on the clipboard rather than in the DOM.

entries.title: Scrape
entries.description: Smart-scrape a list of records from one or more pages, with optional pagination and a maximum-results cap, using axiom.scrape().

entries.title: Scrape metadata
entries.description: Extract page-level metadata (title, analytics IDs, schema.org structured data, or any meta-tag content) using axiom.scrapeMetadata().

entries.title: Integrate AI
entries.description: Run an LLM call inline within a step-function session for prompt completion, classification, or extraction using axiom.integrateAI().

entries.title: Solve captcha
entries.description: Send the current page's captcha to a third-party solver and submit the result using axiom.solveCaptcha().

entries.title: Wait
entries.description: Pause the session for a fixed duration on the pod, keeping the session alive while you wait for content to render or for a server-side process to finish.

entries.title: Restart browser
entries.description: Restart the cloud browser within the current session to recover from a wedged state without losing the session itself, using axiom.restartBrowser().

entries.title: Close a session
entries.description: Close the cloud browser session to free its resources and stop consuming runtime quota.

entries.title: Step function vs No-Code step
entries.description: Find the axiom-api method equivalent of every No-Code Tool step, useful when porting a visual automation to code.

categories.description: Check remaining runtime, understand queueing and concurrency, and stay within rate limits when calling the axiom.ai API.

entries.title: Check remaining runtime
entries.description: Query the axiom.ai API for your account's cloud runtime allowance, used minutes, and remaining minutes for the current billing period.

entries.title: Queue and concurrency
entries.description: Understand how axiom.ai handles concurrent cloud runs, when triggers get queued, and how the queue clears.

entries.title: Usage and limits
entries.description: Check remaining runtime, understand queueing and concurrency, and stay within rate limits when calling the axiom.ai API.

entries.title: Rate limits
entries.description: The axiom.ai API allows 100 calls per minute per API key. Calls past that limit return a 429 error and the requested automation does not run.

entries.description: Reference for the JSON structure of an exported axiom.ai automation, covering identifiers, step data, parameters, settings, and notification fields.

entries.description: A deep dive into how axiom.ai stores cookies for automations, how cookies behave when an automation is duplicated, and why resyncing is sometimes required.

entries.description: Tutorials for automating the most common browser actions, including clicks, text input, file uploads, downloads, tabs, and date pickers.

entries.description: Click buttons in a web app using the Click element step, the Press key(s) step, or custom JavaScript.

entries.description: Click many buttons at once with Click multiple elements, loop through CSS selectors, or use jump steps to repeat a click.

entries.description: Use the Enter text step to type into form fields, with optional support for line breaks and dynamic data.

categories.description: Configure how axiom.ai launches Chrome or Chromium, including bot detection bypass, profiles, executable path, extensions, and incognito mode.

entries.title: Bypass bot detection
entries.description: Get past Cloudflare and similar bot blockers by attaching axiom.ai to an existing Chrome window that has already passed the challenge.

entries.title: Chrome
entries.description: Configure how axiom.ai launches Chrome or Chromium, including bot detection bypass, profiles, executable path, extensions, and incognito mode.

entries.title: Set executable path
entries.description: Point axiom.ai at your local Chrome installation so automations run in Chrome instead of the built-in Chromium browser.

entries.title: Set a custom Chromium profile
entries.description: Point axiom.ai at a custom Chromium profile so automations carry over browser settings, downloads behaviour, and other preferences.

entries.title: Load another extension
entries.description: Load a Chrome extension into the Chromium window axiom.ai uses, with one extension at a time and known limitations on interactivity.

entries.title: Run in incognito mode
entries.description: Force axiom.ai to run an automation in a fresh incognito window with no stored cookies, useful when sessions need to start clean.

categories.description: Configure how an automation reacts to errors, slow-loading pages, and iframes using continue on error, page monitoring, and iframe support.

entries.title: Continue on error
entries.description: Toggle Continue on error to keep an automation running past failures instead of stopping, with errors shown as warnings in run reports.

entries.title: Error handling
entries.description: Configure how an automation reacts to errors, slow-loading pages, and iframes using continue on error, page monitoring, and iframe support.

entries.title: Interact with iframes
entries.description: Enable iframe support so an automation can scrape and click inside embedded iframes, which axiom.ai ignores by default.

entries.title: Disable page monitoring
entries.description: Disable page monitoring so axiom.ai stops waiting for page changes between steps, useful when monitoring slows automations significantly.

categories.description: Download an automation as a JSON file to share or back up, or import a previously downloaded automation into the Builder.

entries.title: Import and export
entries.description: Download an automation as a JSON file to share or back up, or import a previously downloaded automation into the Builder.

entries.title: Import or download an automation
entries.description: Import a previously downloaded automation as a JSON file, or download an automation as JSON to share or back up.

categories.description: Configure how an automation runs, including scheduling, proxies, timezone, max runtime, headless mode, notifications, and stored cookies.

entries.title: Run options
entries.description: Configure how an automation runs, including scheduling, proxies, timezone, max runtime, headless mode, notifications, and stored cookies.

entries.title: Schedule an automation
entries.description: Run an automation on a recurring schedule by setting a start date, time, and frequency, with optional JavaScript conditions for advanced patterns.

entries.title: Use a proxy
entries.description: Route automation traffic through a proxy to bypass bot blocks or geo-restricted content, with guidance on parsing a proxy URL into the right fields.

entries.title: Configure timezone
entries.description: Set the timezone used by scheduled cloud automations, instead of the default UTC.

entries.title: Set up notifications
entries.description: Receive email or webhook notifications when an automation fails, finishes with warnings, or completes successfully.

entries.title: Store cookies
entries.description: Share browser cookies and local storage with cloud runs so logged-in sessions are reused, with optional auto-detection of URLs from your automation.

entries.title: Set maximum runtime
entries.description: Set a maximum runtime so an automation stops automatically if it runs longer than expected.

entries.title: Run headless
entries.description: Run an automation in headless mode so it executes in the background without opening a visible browser window.

entries.title: Concurrency
entries.description: Allow more than one instance of an automation to run at the same time on the cloud or desktop, or keep them sequential when order matters.

entries.title: Block resources
entries.description: Block images, fonts, scripts, and other webpage resources during a run to cut bandwidth and speed up automations.

categories.description: Every step available in axiom.ai's no-code browser automation builder, grouped by what it does.

entries.title: Step index
entries.description: Every step available in axiom.ai's no-code browser automation builder, grouped by what it does.

entries.title: Add error metadata step
entries.description: Insert debug or error messages. Use to test data output and check step results during automation runs.

entries.title: Add row numbers
entries.description: Add row numbers to your data so you can track, organise, and manipulate each row during your automation.

entries.title: Append or Merge data step
entries.description: Join data from separate steps. Use to combine scraped data with data from a Google Sheet. Join single columns or entire datasets.

entries.title: Back step
entries.description: Go back to the previous tab. Return to an earlier page during an automation run.

entries.title: Clear cookies step
entries.description: Clear cookies during a run. Use to log out or fix login issues caused by corrupted cookies.

entries.title: Clear data from a Google Sheet step
entries.description: Clear data from your Google Sheet. Use to remove rows, columns, or entire ranges before writing new data.

entries.title: Click element step
entries.description: Replicate page clicks on links or buttons with the Click element step. Point and click to select the action you want to automate.

entries.title: Click multiple elements step
entries.description: Replicate multiple clicks on links or buttons with the Click element step. Point and click to select each action to automate.

entries.title: Click social media buttons step
entries.description: Automate actions on social media such as like and follow. Set a value to ensure clicks do not unfollow or unlike.

entries.title: Close browser tab step
entries.description: Close tabs during runtime. Use to manage open pages and keep the automation focused on active tasks.

entries.title: Conditionally jump to another step
entries.description: Jump to a different step based on logic set. Use strings, numbers, or JavaScript to define your argument.

entries.title: Continue only if a condition is met step
entries.description: Control bot runs with logic. Decide to continue or end based on argument constructed. Use data passed by other steps. Build argument with strings, numbers, or JavaScript.

entries.title: Count rows step
entries.description: Count number of rows in a Google Sheet. Returns a variable for use in steps like Read data from Google Sheet to control how many rows to loop through or how many steps to jump.

entries.title: Create a new Google Sheet step
entries.description: Create new Google Sheet on the fly during a bot run. Step returns the sheet value for use in other steps.

entries.title: Date and time step
entries.description: Use this step to return a date and time value as a token in your web automation.

entries.title: Date picker step
entries.description: A step designed to automate date pickers, commonly found in web forms. Select day, month, and year.

entries.title: Delete rows from a Google Sheet step
entries.description: Delete rows from a Google Sheet. Use with Find rows in Google Sheet to locate and remove specific cells or rows.

entries.title: Display a message step
entries.description: Use Display message to open a dialog window in the browser. Pass data from other steps by inserting their tokens. Useful for debugging.

entries.title: Download a file to Google Drive step
entries.description: Download files from a web page directly to your Google Drive. Use this step to download files when running bots in the cloud.

entries.title: Download file from URL step
entries.description: Use this step to automatically download a file from a specified URL to your computer.

entries.title: Download file step
entries.description: Download a file via the desktop app to the local drive where the app is installed.

entries.title: Download files step
entries.description: Download files via the desktop app to the local drive where the app is installed.

entries.title: Edit Row step
entries.description: This step combines Find row and Write to Google Sheet. Use to locate a row and overwrite its data.

entries.title: End loop
entries.description: Learn how to use the End loop step in your Axiom

entries.title: End run
entries.description: Use this step to end a run. Stops all remaining steps in the automation.

entries.title: Enter custom data step
entries.description: Insert custom data returned for use in your automation. Used to store small amounts of data for use in your automations.

entries.title: Enter text step
entries.description: Use this step to enter text into text fields found in web forms. Pass data from other steps into the Enter text step to automate data entry.

entries.title: Export to CSV step
entries.description: Export any data from Axiom in the form of a CSV. Use to download structured results for use outside the automation.

entries.title: Generate random password step
entries.description: Generate random password. Use in automations that require secure input like account creation or form filling.

entries.title: Get a list of links from a page step
entries.description: Scrape links from a webpage. Return results via a token for use in your automation.

entries.title: Get a list of links from a URL step
entries.description: Scrape links from a URL. Return results via a token for use in your automation.

entries.title: Get current bot URL step
entries.description: Fetch URL from the active tab during a bot run. Return it for use in your automation.

entries.title: Get data from a URL
entries.description: Scrape data from a URL including images, HTML, and text. Point and click to select the data you wish to extract.

entries.title: Get data from a webpage
entries.description: Scrape data from a webpage including images, HTML, and text. Point and click to select the data you wish to extract.

entries.title: Go to page
entries.description: Load a web page. Pass tokens into this step to retrieve URLs from data sources like spreadsheets.

entries.title: If a condition is true, run steps
entries.description: Execute a set of steps depending on an If condition. Pass data into this step to use when constructing your argument. IF Conditions can use text, numbers, or custom JavaScript.

entries.title: If Else condition step
entries.description: Execute a set of steps depending on an If Else condition. Pass data into this step to use when constructing your argument. IF Conditions can use text, numbers, or custom JavaScript.

entries.title: Import CSV File step
entries.description: Import data from a CSV. Pass to other steps via a token for use in your automation.

entries.title: Integrate AI
entries.description: Interact with LLMs to extract data, generate text or make decisions. Supports either ChatGPT, Claude and others, requires an API key.

entries.title: Interact step
entries.description: This step is now defunct. Replaced by the Loop step.

entries.title: Join different data sources based on matching columns step
entries.description: Merge data from multiple sources into a single dataset by matching columns. Used to combine data from large, database-style sets.

entries.title: Jump to another step
entries.description: Automations run steps in sequence. Use this step to break that sequence and jump to a step outside the order.

entries.title: Loop step
entries.description: Loop through sub-steps inside a loop. Set data to loop through, such as URLs in a Google Sheet. Pass data into sub-steps using tokens.

entries.title: Mouse click and drag
entries.description: Replicate dragging objects with a mouse by recording coordinates. Use to automate drag-and-drop actions on a page.

entries.title: Move a Google Sheet to a new folder in Google Drive
entries.description: Used to move a Google Sheet within your Google Drive account.

entries.title: Press keys(s) step
entries.description: Record keyboard input. Include actions like pressing Return to submit a form or Tab to move between fields. Used to replay typed input as part of an automation.

entries.title: Read data from a Google Sheet step
entries.description: Fetch data from a Google Sheet to use in your automation. Pass it into steps using tokens.

entries.title: Read data from Airtable
entries.description: Import data from Airtable into your automations.

entries.title: Read data from Excel sheet step
Read file from Google Drive step
Receive data from another app
entries.description: Use the 'receive data from another app' step to trigger your automation via our API and webhooks, an integration or a third-party service such as Postman, Power Automate, Google Apps Scripts and much more.

entries.title: Remove duplicates step
entries.description: Check and remove duplicate data from sources such as Google Sheets or CSV files. Use to clean and prepare data before use.

entries.title: Remove HTML code step
entries.description: Strip HTML from a data source, including data returned by Get data steps. Use to clean text before using it in your automation.

entries.title: Remove results that contain certain words step
entries.description: Filter data by words they contain. Works with multiple sources including Google Sheets and Get data steps.

entries.title: Remove results which do not contain certain words step
entries.description: Filter data by words they do not contain. Works with multiple sources including Google Sheets and Get data steps.

entries.title: Replace text step
entries.description: Replace text from any of your data sources by inserting their token in this step. Leave blank to delete, or add text to replace with.

entries.title: Restart browser
entries.description: Learn how to use the Restart browser step in your Axiom

entries.title: Rollover element step
entries.description: Replicate hovering over HTML elements. Use when you need to reveal hidden data before scraping.

entries.title: Run another Axiom step
entries.description: Run other Axioms from inside a single Axiom with this step. Use to chain automations or reuse existing bots.

entries.title: Save page HTML step
entries.description: Save snapshots of a page’s HTML to your Google Drive. Use to archive content or review page structure later.

entries.title: Save screenshot locally step
entries.description: Save screenshots to your local Drive. Use to archive content or review page structure later.

entries.title: Save screenshot to Google Drive step
entries.description: Save screenshots to your gogole Drive. Use to archive content or review page structure later.

entries.title: Select list step
entries.description: Automate interactions with select lists and dropdowns. Pass data into the step to dynamically select options.

entries.title: Select random rows step
entries.description: Randomly select a row from a Google Sheet. Use this step to pick a single entry for actions like testing, sampling, or random output.

entries.title: Send an email step
entries.description: Send an email. Use to share scraped data, distribute downloaded files, or confirm that an Axiom has completed.

entries.title: Solve Captcha step
entries.description: Use a third party to solve captchas encountered during your bot run.

entries.title: Split a name into columns step
entries.description: Extract first names, middle names, and surnames from scraped data. Pass in a token from your selected data source to use.

entries.title: Split by character step
entries.description: Extract data from large volumes of scraped text using the Split by character step. Use multiple steps if needed to remove text before the start and after the end.

entries.title: Switch browser tab step
entries.description: Use this step to switch active browser tabs during a run. Helps control which page the automation interacts with.

entries.title: Trigger webhook step
entries.description: Post data to other web apps via webhook.

entries.title: Try catch
entries.description: Execute a different set of steps if an error is encountered during runtime. Use to handle errors.

entries.title: Upload a file from Google Drive step
entries.description: Upload files directly to a folder in your Google Drive.

entries.title: Upload a file step
entries.description: Using the desktop app, upload files locally from the computer where the app is installed.

entries.title: Wait step
entries.description: Pause your automation to manually interact or wait for an event to conclude before continuing.

entries.title: Write data to a Excel sheet step
Write data to a Google Sheet step
entries.description: Output data from your automation to a Google Sheet.

entries.title: Write data to Airtable
entries.description: Export data to Airtable from your automations.

entries.title: Write javascript step
entries.description: Use JavaScript and Puppeteer in your no-code automation to extend functionality. Puppeteer library is already loaded, no need to include it.

categories.description: See what's new in the latest axiom.ai release, plus the full archive of previous releases covering features, fixes, and improvements.

entries.title: Release notes
entries.description: See what's new in the latest axiom.ai release, plus the full archive of previous releases covering features, fixes, and improvements.

entries.title: Affiliates, smarter queues, overwrite files, and a 4.0 teaser
Axiom academy is live, smarter scraping, improved validation
Better builder errors, webhook stability, test default data, and more
Better notifications, custom errors, iframe tools, and more
Better sheets integration, loop controls, and fixes
Build smarter with nesting, chatgpt 4, and cloud sessions
Custom scraper settings, new templates, and payment upgrade
Dashboard redesign, better conditionals, stop remote bots, and more
Excel steps, folder sorting, GPT-4o smarts, end loop control, quick filters, and more
Guided templates, step help, datepickers, partial screenshots, and more
Last stop before 4.0, Scrape images, reorder columns, and more
Live data previews, easier navigation, new onboarding tools
Live run status, step recommendations, keyboard shortcuts, and more
New debugger, generate text with chatgpt, save to drive, and more
New Make integration, better loops, smarter scraping, and fixes
Optional steps, screenshot tool, scraper upgrades
Proxy auth, conditional logic, flexible scheduling, and more
Proxy rotation, AI updates, new run options and more
Quick builder, new sidebar, better onboarding, and more
Scrape faster, set keys once, skip steps, end runs early, and more
Selector overhaul, drag to reorder steps, new templates, and more
Sneak peek at 4.0, open linked axioms, new steps, and more
Solve captchas, press keys smarter, fix iframe hangs, and more
Step-by-step templates, improved scraping, click multiple, and more
Structured scraping with chatgpt, new schedule view, filters, and fixes
Upload to drive, undo mistakes, upgrade steps, and more

categories.description: Get started with the axiom.ai No-Code Tool. Install the Chrome extension, create an account, and build your first browser automation.

entries.title: Make automations with our no-code tool
entries.description: Get started with the axiom.ai No-Code Tool. Install the Chrome extension, create an account, and build your first browser automation.

entries.title: Installation
entries.description: Install the axiom.ai Chrome extension and optional desktop application to build and run browser automations.

entries.title: The dashboard
entries.description: A tour of the axiom.ai dashboard, where you create new automations, check run reports, and find your scheduled automations.

entries.title: The no-code Builder
entries.description: Learn how to add, duplicate, move, and configure steps in the axiom.ai Builder, plus useful keyboard shortcuts.

entries.title: Pass data between steps
entries.description: Learn how data variables work in axiom.ai and how to pass data from one step to another in your automation.

entries.title: Share an automation
entries.description: Download your axiom.ai automation as a template file so you can share it with our support team or with other users.

entries.title: AI Assistant
entries.description: Use the in-Builder AI Assistant to debug failing automations, get suggested fixes, extend your automation with new steps, and resolve common configuration issues from a chat conversation.

categories.description: Use the no-code selector tool to pick elements to interact with, extract data from a page, and pass custom CSS selectors into your automations.

entries.title: Use the Single Selector
entries.description: Use the no-code Single Selector to point and click an element on a page, type a text label to match it, paste a custom CSS selector, or pass selectors in from a data source.

entries.title: Use the Multi Selector tool
entries.description: Point and click to select text, images, links, and HTML for extraction, group repeating content into rows, and apply custom CSS selectors.

entries.title: Use custom CSS selectors
entries.description: Set custom CSS selectors in the Single Selector and Multi Selector tools to target elements that the default picker can't reach.

entries.title: The selector tool
entries.description: Use the no-code selector tool to pick elements to interact with, extract data from a page, and pass custom CSS selectors into your automations.

categories.description: Tools and patterns for debugging an axiom.ai automation, handling errors gracefully, and finding the cause when a run fails.

entries.title: Debug an automation
entries.description: Use the in-builder error messages, debugger, run reports, and steps like Display a message and Add error metadata to find the cause of a failing automation.

entries.title: Handle errors in an automation
entries.description: Catch errors with Try / Catch, ignore them with Continue on error, handle empty loops gracefully, and get notified when runs fail.

entries.title: Troubleshooting
entries.description: Tools and patterns for debugging an axiom.ai automation, handling errors gracefully, and finding the cause when a run fails.

categories.description: Browse common errors by category to find the cause and recommended fix for issues with installation, runs, and the desktop application.

entries.title: Common errors
entries.description: Browse common errors by category to find the cause and recommended fix for issues with installation, runs, and the desktop application.

entries.title: Installation and uninstallation errors
entries.description: Errors that can appear when installing or uninstalling the axiom.ai Chrome extension or desktop application, and how to resolve them.

entries.title: General errors
entries.description: Errors that can occur during automation runs, including server errors, version mismatches, concurrency limits, and browser launch failures.

entries.title: Runtime errors
entries.description: Errors that occur while an automation is running, including missing elements, browser navigation issues, and runtime limits on long jobs.

entries.title: Desktop app errors
entries.description: Errors that can occur with the axiom.ai desktop application, including port conflicts that prevent the app from starting.

categories.description: Browse errors specific to axiom.ai integrations including Google Sheets, AI providers, and 2Captcha, with the cause and recommended fix for each.

entries.title: Google Sheets errors
entries.description: Errors that can occur when reading from or writing to Google Sheets, including expired tokens, missing data, permission issues, and invalid ranges.

entries.title: AI integration errors
entries.description: Errors that can occur when using AI steps with OpenAI, Google Gemini, Anthropic Claude, or Perplexity Sonar, mostly related to missing or invalid API keys.

entries.title: Integration errors
entries.description: Browse errors specific to axiom.ai integrations including Google Sheets, AI providers, and 2Captcha, with the cause and recommended fix for each.

entries.title: 2Captcha errors
entries.description: Errors that can occur when using the Solve Captcha step with 2Captcha, mostly related to missing or invalid API keys.

categories.description: Browse errors specific to axiom.ai steps by category, including interact, scrape, navigate, loop, files, control flow, JavaScript, and webhooks.

entries.title: Interact step errors
entries.description: Errors that can occur with interact steps including click, enter text, and press key, with the cause and recommended fix for each.

entries.title: Scrape step errors
entries.description: Errors that can occur with scrape steps including Get data from a URL and Get data from current page, with the cause and recommended fix.

entries.title: Step errors
entries.description: Browse errors specific to axiom.ai steps by category, including interact, scrape, navigate, loop, files, control flow, JavaScript, and webhooks.

entries.title: Navigate step errors
entries.description: Errors that can occur with navigate steps including Go to page and Switch browser tab, with the cause and recommended fix for each.

entries.title: Loop step errors
entries.description: Errors that can occur with loop steps, including how to debug a loop where every iteration fails.

entries.title: File step errors
entries.description: Errors that can occur with file steps including Upload a file and Download file, with the cause and recommended fix for each.

entries.title: Control flow step errors
entries.description: Errors that can occur with control flow steps including Jump to another step and Continue only if a condition is met.

entries.title: Other step errors
entries.description: Errors that don't fit other step categories, including JavaScript runtime errors from the Write javascript step.

entries.title: Webhook and API step errors
entries.description: Errors that can occur when using the Trigger webhook and Receive data from another app steps, including JSON validation issues and missing test data.
